# How algorithmic confounding in recommendation systems increases homogeneity and decreases utility

**Authors:** Allison J. B. Chaney, Brandon M. Stewart, Barbara E. Engelhardt  
**Year:** 2018  
**URL:** https://bstewart.scholar.princeton.edu/publications/how-algorithmic-confounding-recommendation-systems-increases-homogeneity-and  
**Evidence type:** Simulation / recommender-systems research

## Claim supported

Recommendation systems trained on behaviour shaped by previous recommendations can create feedback loops.

## Key result

Simulations demonstrate pernicious feedback loops and homogenization without utility gains.

## How it supports manufactured traction

This source strengthens the argument that digital systems can be moved by manipulated proxy signals. Use it as evidence for the mechanism, not as proof that every campaign succeeds.
